faqs.question: How much do classes cost?
faqs.answer: The annual contribution is $210 per class for the year. Classes are heavily subsidised by the Ministry of Education. Instrument hire is additional: violins $80/year, cellos $100/year.

faqs.question: When and where are classes held?
faqs.answer: All classes are on Saturday mornings at Dyer Street School, 9 Dyer Street, Epuni, Lower Hutt, between 8:30am and 12:30pm. Each class is 30 minutes long.

faqs.question: What age does my child need to be?
faqs.answer: Most instrument classes start at age 7. Music Basics is for ages 5–6. Ukulele starts at 6.5, trumpet at 8+, and flute at around 9+.

faqs.question: Does my child need their own instrument?
faqs.answer: Students are expected to have an instrument to play at home and bring it to class each week. Gallery Music Centre has violins available for hire ($80/year) and cellos ($100/year). Tutors can advise on purchasing guitars, keyboards, ukuleles, recorders, and drum practice pads. Trumpets and flutes can be hired from Lower Hutt music shops. Recorders are an inexpensive first instrument (c.$40 for book and recorder).

faqs.question: How are class placements decided?
faqs.answer: Priority goes to students who have done music basics, then to siblings of existing students and families from Dyer Street School. Students will be placed in the class best suited to their level.

faqs.question: What about music books and materials?
faqs.answer: Tutor books are chosen by each tutor and are usually available from them directly or from local music stores. This is an additional cost beyond the class contribution.

faqs.question: Is there a waitlist?
faqs.answer: Some classes (especially keyboard and drums) have waitlists. Beginners can only start instrumental classes in February each year, so enquiries after that date can choose to go on the waitlist for the following year. Students with prior learning are welcome to get in touch about joining anytime during the year. Choir, orchestra, ensemble and music basics can be joined at any time.

faqs.question: Can my child learn more than one instrument?
faqs.answer: Students wishing to learn more than one instrument will be placed in additional classes if spaces are available.

faqs.question: What is the annual concert?
faqs.answer: Gallery holds an annual concert (usually in Term 3) where students perform for family and friends. It’s a highlight of the year and a wonderful chance to make music with others and celebrate the progress every student has made.

faqs.question: How do I enrol?
faqs.answer: Click on 'Enrol' and fill out the form, or email gallerymusiccentre@gmail.com
